patches.summary: Wrap Surf's Chrome-fingerprinted transport with MFPTransport in newHTTPClient so all spec-emitted commands clear MFP's anti-bot.
patches.reason: MFP redirects stdlib User-Agent requests to /account/login even with valid cookies. Surf's TLS fingerprint matches a real Chrome and clears the challenge; MFPTransport must remain as the outer wrapper for /v2/ host rewriting and mfp-* headers.
patches.validated_outcome: Live diary get-day, water, exercise, food search, and user auth-token all return real account data instead of the login-page redirect they hit before.

patches.summary: Emit a loud stderr warning when Surf's underlying *http.Transport is nil instead of silently falling back to http.DefaultTransport.
patches.reason: If a future enetx/surf release changes Std()'s shape and returns a client with no Transport, the silent fallback in surf-transport-shared-client would lose the Chrome TLS fingerprint and every MFP request would hit anti-bot with no visible cause. The warning makes the failure mode debuggable.

patches.summary: Send the user's cookie payload as Cookie: header, not Authorization:.
patches.reason: MFP's web routes (/food/diary, /goals, /exercise, etc.) authenticate via cookie. Sending the cookie string as Authorization: causes MFP to treat the request as unauthenticated. The generator default targets bearer-auth; cookie auth needed the explicit override here.

patches.summary: --dry-run prints `Cookie:` (not `Authorization:`) so its output matches the live request shape.
patches.reason: The dryRun helper hard-coded `Authorization` as the auth-line label, but cookie-header-not-authorization made the live path send `Cookie:`. A user running --dry-run to diagnose auth failures would see the wrong header and chase the exact bug this PR fixes.

patches.summary: Remove the post-overrides User-Agent assignment that pinned the request to myfitnesspal-pp-cli/0.1.0.
patches.reason: Surf's Impersonate().Chrome() sets the right Chrome User-Agent at the TLS layer; the subsequent req.Header.Set('User-Agent', 'myfitnesspal-pp-cli/0.1.0') was overwriting it and re-triggering bot detection on every request that went through the shared client.

patches.summary: Hand-written fetchAuthenticatedHTML helper now uses Surf's Chrome-fingerprinted *http.Client.
patches.reason: Hand-written diary_get_day.go bypasses the shared client and previously made its own stdlib http call that hit the same anti-bot wall. Routing through Surf brings the diary scrape path in line with the rest of the CLI.
patches.validated_outcome: Yesterday's diary returns parsed JSON with real meal entries.

patches.summary: Promote the Surf-backed *http.Client in diary_get_day.go to a package-level sync.Once singleton so multi-date reads reuse TLS connections; extract only Surf's Transport and wrap it in a jar-less *http.Client.
patches.reason: fetchAuthenticatedHTML previously built a fresh Surf client (and its TLS connection pool) on every call, costing a full TLS handshake per date. Reusing the full Surf *http.Client (which carries a CookieJar from Impersonate().Chrome()) would have accumulated Set-Cookie responses from MFP and re-sent them alongside the manually-set Cookie: <session> header on subsequent calls, producing duplicate/conflicting cookies. Mirrors newHTTPClient's pattern in internal/client/client.go.

patches.summary: Emit a loud stderr warning when Surf's underlying *http.Transport is nil in sharedDiaryHTTPClient(), mirroring the existing guard in newHTTPClient.
patches.reason: sharedDiaryHTTPClient previously assigned surfStd.Transport without a nil check. If a future enetx/surf release changes Std()'s shape and returns a client with no Transport, the silent fallback would drop the Chrome TLS fingerprint on the diary scrape path and every request would hit MFP's anti-bot wall with no visible cause. Brings the diary path in line with surf-transport-nil-loud-warning in internal/client/client.go.

patches.summary: Remove the unconditional Accept: text/html,...,application/json;q=0.9 header from the shared do() function.
patches.reason: The shared client is used for both HTML scrape paths and JSON v2 API endpoints. Setting an HTML-preferring Accept header globally would cause any content-negotiating API endpoint (/v2/foods/*, /api/services/reports/*) to return HTML and break JSON parsing. HTML scrape paths set their own Accept where it's actually the target; the Chrome TLS fingerprint (not the Accept header) is what clears MFP's anti-bot.

patches.summary: Prepend www. when the cookie domain begins with . (parent-domain notation) before passing the URL to pycookiecheat.
patches.reason: pycookiecheat's chrome_cookies('https://myfitnesspal.com') returns a different (smaller) cookie set than chrome_cookies('https://www.myfitnesspal.com') — the bare-domain form misses parent-domain cookies, which include the __Secure-next-auth.session-token MFP needs to recognize the request as logged-in. Auth login was reporting success but storing 12 of the wrong cookies.

known_remaining_issues.summary: v2 Bearer-token-required endpoints (/api/services/reports/*, /v2/foods/*, /v2/users/*) still fail with 'Missing HTTP header: Authorization' because the shared client now sends Cookie: but those endpoints need Authorization: Bearer <token> bootstrapped from /user/auth_token.
known_remaining_issues.next_step: Auth-mode-aware client routing: detect v2 paths and use Bearer (cached after one auth-token call) instead of Cookie.

known_remaining_issues.summary: goals command path /account/my-goals returns the login redirect because the actual endpoint is /profile/goals.
known_remaining_issues.next_step: Fix the spec or the promoted-goals.go path.

known_remaining_issues.summary: Diary parser: nutrient field names contain embedded newlines and unit suffixes (e.g. 'calories\n                      kcal').
known_remaining_issues.next_step: Trim whitespace and split out units in internal/parser/diary.go.
